column4: And these are the generations of {{c1::Isaac}}, Abraham’s son: Abraham begot Isaac.
And these are the generations of Isaac, {{c1::Abraham’s}} son: Abraham begot Isaac.
And {{c1::Isaac}} was forty years old when he took Rebekah, the daughter of Bethuel the Aramean, of Paddan-aram, the sister of Laban the Aramean, to be his wife.
And Isaac was forty years old when he took {{c1::Rebekah}}, the daughter of Bethuel the Aramean, of Paddan-aram, the sister of Laban the Aramean, to be his wife.
And {{c1::Isaac}} entreated the LORD for his wife, because she was barren; and the LORD let Himself be entreated of him, and Rebekah his wife conceived.
And Isaac entreated the LORD for his wife, because she was barren; and the LORD let {{c1::Himself}} be entreated of him, and Rebekah his wife conceived.
And the children struggled together within her; and she said: ‘{{c1::If}} it be so, wherefore do I live?’ And she went to inquire of the LORD.
And the children struggled together within her; and she said: ‘If it be so, wherefore do {{c1::I}} live?’ And she went to inquire of the LORD.

And the LORD said unto her: {{c1::Two}} nations are in thy womb, And two peoples shall be separated from thy bowels; And the one people shall be stronger than the other people; And the elder shall serve the younger.

And when her days to be {{c1::delivered}} were fulfilled, behold, there were twins in her womb.

And the first came forth ruddy, all over like a hairy mantle; and they called his name {{c1::Esau}}.
And the first came forth ruddy, all over like a hairy {{c1::mantle}}; and they called his name Esau.
And after that came forth his brother, and his hand had hold on {{c1::Esau’s}} heel; and his name was called Jacob. And Isaac was threescore years old when she bore them.

And the boys grew; and {{c1::Esau}} was a cunning hunter, a man of the field; and Jacob was a quiet man, dwelling in tents.
And the boys grew; and Esau was a cunning hunter, a man of the field; and {{c1::Jacob}} was a quiet man, dwelling in tents.
Now {{c1::Isaac}} loved Esau, because he did eat of his venison; and Rebekah loved Jacob.

And {{c1::Jacob}} sod pottage; and Esau came in from the field, and he was faint.

And {{c1::Esau}} said to Jacob: ‘Let me swallow, I pray thee, some of this red, red pottage; for I am faint.’ Therefore was his name called Edom.
And Esau said to {{c1::Jacob}}: ‘Let me swallow, I pray thee, some of this red, red pottage; for I am faint.’ Therefore was his name called Edom.
And {{c1::Jacob}} said: ‘Sell me first thy birth right.’

And {{c1::Jacob}} said: ‘Swear to me first’; and he swore unto him; and he sold his birthright unto Jacob.

And {{c1::Jacob}} gave Esau bread and pottage of lentils; and he did eat and drink, and rose up, and went his way. So Esau despised his birthright.

And there was a famine in the land, beside the first famine that was in the days of {{c1::Abraham}}. And Isaac went unto Abimelech king of the Philistines unto Gerar.
And there was a famine in the land, beside the first famine that was in the days of Abraham. And {{c1::Isaac}} went unto Abimelech king of the Philistines unto Gerar.
And the LORD appeared unto him, and said: ‘{{c1::Go}} not down unto Egypt; dwell in the land which I shall tell thee of.
And the LORD appeared unto him, and said: ‘Go not down unto {{c1::Egypt}}; dwell in the land which I shall tell thee of.

Sojourn in this land, and {{c1::I}} will be with thee, and will bless thee; for unto thee, and unto thy seed, I will give all these lands, and I will establish the oath which I swore unto Abraham thy father;

and {{c1::I}} will multiply thy seed as the stars of heaven, and will give unto thy seed all these lands; and by thy seed shall all the nations of the earth bless themselves;

because that {{c1::Abraham}} hearkened to My voice, and kept My charge, My commandments, My statutes, and My laws.’
because that Abraham hearkened to {{c1::My}} voice, and kept My charge, My commandments, My statutes, and My laws.’
And {{c1::Isaac}} dwelt in Gerar.
And Isaac dwelt in {{c1::Gerar}}.

And the men of the place asked him of his wife; and he said: ‘{{c1::She}} is my sister’; for he feared to say: ‘My wife’; ‘lest the men of the place should kill me for Rebekah, because she is fair to look upon.’

And it came to pass, when he had been there a long time, that {{c1::Abimelech}} king of the Philistines looked out at a window, and saw, and, behold, Isaac was sporting with Rebekah his wife.
And it came to pass, when he had been there a long time, that Abimelech king of the {{c1::Philistines}} looked out at a window, and saw, and, behold, Isaac was sporting with Rebekah his wife.
And {{c1::Abimelech}} called Isaac, and said: ‘Behold, of a surety she is thy wife; and how saidst thou: She is my sister?’ And Isaac said unto him: ‘Because I said: Lest I die because of her.’
And Abimelech called {{c1::Isaac}}, and said: ‘Behold, of a surety she is thy wife; and how saidst thou: She is my sister?’ And Isaac said unto him: ‘Because I said: Lest I die because of her.’
And {{c1::Abimelech}} said: ‘What is this thou hast done unto us? one of the people might easily have lain with thy wife, and thou wouldest have brought guiltiness upon us.’

And {{c1::Isaac}} sowed in that land, and found in the same year a hundred-fold; and the LORD blessed him.
And Isaac sowed in that land, and found in the same year a {{c1::hundred}}-fold; and the LORD blessed him.
And the man waxed great, and grew more and more until he {{c1::became}} very great.
And he had possessions of flocks, and possessions of herds, and a great household; and the {{c1::Philistines}} envied him.
And he had {{c1::possessions}} of flocks, and possessions of herds, and a great household; and the Philistines envied him.
Now all the wells which his father’s servants had digged in the days of {{c1::Abraham}} his father, the Philistines had stopped them, and filled them with earth.

And {{c1::Abimelech}} said unto Isaac: ‘Go from us; for thou art much mightier than we.’
And Abimelech said unto {{c1::Isaac}}: ‘Go from us; for thou art much mightier than we.’
And {{c1::Isaac}} departed thence, and encamped in the valley of Gerar, and dwelt there.
And Isaac departed thence, and encamped in the valley of {{c1::Gerar}}, and dwelt there.

And {{c1::Isaac}} digged again the wells of water, which they had digged in the days of Abraham his father; for the Philistines had stopped them after the death of Abraham; and he called their names after the names by which his father had called them.

And {{c1::Isaac’s}} servants digged in the valley, and found there a well of living water.

And the herdmen of {{c1::Gerar}} strove with Isaac’s herdmen, saying: ‘The water is ours.’ And he called the name of the well Esek; because they contended with him.
And the herdmen of Gerar strove with {{c1::Isaac’s}} herdmen, saying: ‘The water is ours.’ And he called the name of the well Esek; because they contended with him.
And they digged another well, and they strove for that also. And he called the name of it {{c1::Sitnah}}.
And they {{c1::digged}} another well, and they strove for that also. And he called the name of it Sitnah.

And he removed from thence, and digged another well; and for that they strove not. And he called the name of it {{c1::Rehoboth}}; and he said: ‘For now the LORD hath made room for us, and we shall be fruitful in the land.’

And he went up from thence to {{c1::Beer}}-sheba.
And he went up from {{c1::thence}} to Beer-sheba.

And the LORD appeared unto him the same night, and said: ‘{{c1::I}} am the God of Abraham thy father. Fear not, for I am with thee, and will bless thee, and multiply thy seed for My servant Abraham’s sake.’

that thou wilt do us no hurt, as we have not {{c1::touched}} thee, and as we have done unto thee nothing but good, and have sent thee away in peace; thou art now the blessed of the LORD.’
